openmeter/entitlement/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing entitlement.EntitlementRepo, balanceworker.BalanceWorkerRepository, and meteredentitlement.UsageResetRepo — the sole persistence layer for entitlement rows, usage-reset records, and balance-worker ingest-event queries.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method
patterns.description: Every public method body wraps with entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound Ent transaction is honored. Never call repo.db.* outside the wrapper closure.
patterns.example: entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx, repo *entitlementDBAdapter) (*entitlement.Entitlement, error) { return repo.mapEntitlementEntity(...) })

patterns.name: TxUser+TxCreator triad in transaction.go
patterns.description: Both entitlementDBAdapter and usageResetDBAdapter implement Tx()/WithTx()/Self() in transaction.go. A new adapter struct missing these makes TransactingRepo panic.
patterns.example: func (e *entitlementDBAdapter) WithTx(ctx, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *entitlementDBAdapter { return NewPostgresEntitlementRepo(db.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()).Client()) }

patterns.name: Soft-delete filter on every query
patterns.description: Active entitlements exclude soft-deleted rows via db_entitlement.Or(DeletedAtGT(now), DeletedAtIsNil()). Omitting returns deleted rows and breaks billing correctness.
patterns.example: Where(db_entitlement.Or(db_entitlement.DeletedAtGT(clock.Now()), db_entitlement.DeletedAtIsNil()))

patterns.name: withAllUsageResets eager-load
patterns.description: Any query feeding mapEntitlementEntity must call withAllUsageResets to eager-load usage_reset edges, or UsagePeriod computation fails.
patterns.example: withAllUsageResets(repo.db.Entitlement.Query(), []string{namespace}).Where(...).First(ctx)

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertions
patterns.description: var _ repo = (*entitlementDBAdapter)(nil) and var _ interface{transaction.Creator; entutils.TxUser[...]} guard compliance. Add for every new adapter type.
patterns.example: var _ repo = (*entitlementDBAdapter)(nil)

patterns.name: Raw SQL only for complex cross-table CTEs
patterns.description: ListEntitlementsAffectedByIngestEvents uses EntitlementsByIngestedEventsQuery raw SQL because it joins customer/subject/feature/meter via CTEs. All other queries use Ent builders.
patterns.example: query, args := EntitlementsByIngestedEventsQuery(repo.db.GetConfig().Driver.Dialect(), ns, subject, meters...); rows, err := repo.db.QueryContext(ctx, query, args...)

patterns.name: Reload edges after Create
patterns.description: CreateEntitlement does a second Query().Only(ctx) after Save() to reload customer/subject edges before mapEntitlementEntity. Replicate for other create methods.
patterns.example: res, _ := cmd.Save(ctx); entWithEdges, _ := repo.db.Entitlement.Query().Where(db_entitlement.ID(res.ID)).Only(ctx)

key_file_guides.role: Main adapter: all EntitlementRepo + BalanceWorkerRepository method implementations using Ent query builders plus the raw-SQL CTE query.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New methods must wrap in TransactingRepo and call withAllUsageResets when feeding mapEntitlementEntity.

key_file_guides.role: Tx/WithTx/Self for both entitlementDBAdapter and usageResetDBAdapter via HijackTx + NewTxDriver.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New adapter struct in this package needs all three methods copied or it cannot join ctx transactions.

key_file_guides.role: usageResetDBAdapter.Save writes a usage_reset row inside a transaction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always call usageResetTime.Validate() before the Ent create call; do not bypass.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests provisioning real Postgres via testutils.InitPostgresDB, constructing adapters directly from NewPostgresEntitlementRepo.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() — acceptable in tests only, never copy into production methods.

anti_patterns: Calling repo.db.* outside a TransactingRepo wrapper.
Omitting withAllUsageResets on a query that feeds mapEntitlementEntity.
Importing app/common in test files — construct adapters directly.
Adding business logic to the adapter — keep it pure persistence.
Using context.Background() in production adapter methods.

decisions.decision: Two separate adapter structs (entitlementDBAdapter, usageResetDBAdapter) each with their own TxUser.
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions are client-scoped; splitting keeps each rebind path small and unrelated edges separate.

decisions.decision: Soft-delete via DeletedAt timestamp rather than hard delete.
decisions.rationale: Entitlement history is needed for billing reconciliation and grant burn-down replay; hard delete destroys the audit trail.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a write method to entitlementDBAdapter

openmeter/entitlement/boolean.purpose: Boolean entitlement sub-type implementing entitlement.SubTypeConnector for on/off feature-flag access — carries no balance or usage tracking and GetValue always returns HasAccess=true.

patterns.name: SubTypeConnector three-method implementation
patterns.description: Implement exactly GetValue, BeforeCreate, AfterCreate (no-op). Do not add a fourth method.
patterns.example: func (c *connector) AfterCreate(ctx context.Context, entitlement *entitlement.Entitlement) error { return nil }

patterns.name: ParseFromGenericEntitlement type guard first
patterns.description: GetValue calls ParseFromGenericEntitlement immediately; it checks EntitlementType==EntitlementTypeBoolean and returns WrongTypeError otherwise.
patterns.example: _, err := ParseFromGenericEntitlement(entitlement); if err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: BeforeCreate computes currentUsagePeriod when UsagePeriod set
patterns.description: Boolean entitlements support UsagePeriod for scheduling; BeforeCreate calculates currentUsagePeriod via usagePeriod.GetValue().GetPeriodAt(clock.Now()) when non-nil.
patterns.example: calculatedPeriod, err := usagePeriod.GetValue().GetPeriodAt(clock.Now()); currentUsagePeriod = &calculatedPeriod

patterns.name: Reject metered-only fields in BeforeCreate
patterns.description: BeforeCreate returns InvalidValueError if MeasureUsageFrom, IssueAfterReset, IsSoftLimit, or Config are set. UsagePeriod is explicitly allowed.
patterns.example: if model.MeasureUsageFrom != nil || model.IssueAfterReset != nil || model.IsSoftLimit != nil || model.Config != nil { return nil, &entitlement.InvalidValueError{...} }

key_file_guides.role: Full SubTypeConnector impl. GetValue returns BooleanEntitlementValue{} (HasAccess()==true). BeforeCreate rejects metered-only fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UsagePeriod IS allowed for boolean entitlements — do not add it to the rejection list.

key_file_guides.role: Entitlement struct (embeds GenericProperties only) and ParseFromGenericEntitlement type-narrowing function.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add balance/usage fields — the struct is intentionally empty beyond GenericProperties.

anti_patterns: Adding balance or usage fields to the boolean Entitlement struct.
Returning HasAccess=false from BooleanEntitlementValue.
Calling a credit engine or grant repo from AfterCreate — boolean entitlements have no grants.
Adding MeasureUsageFrom/IssueAfterReset to the BeforeCreate acceptance list.

decisions.decision: Boolean entitlement always returns HasAccess=true without credit computation.
decisions.rationale: Boolean entitlements are pure feature flags with no credit burn-down; access is true as long as the entitlement is active.

openmeter/entitlement/metered.purpose: Metered entitlement sub-type implementing credit-backed grant burn-down via the credit engine and ClickHouse usage queries — owns balance queries, usage resets, grant lifecycle, and the grant.OwnerConnector bridge.

patterns.name: Constructor wires all credit sub-systems
patterns.description: NewMeteredEntitlementConnector takes streaming/owner/balance/grant connectors, repos, publisher, logger, tracer (all Wire-provided). Never instantiate directly in production.
patterns.example: meteredentitlement.NewMeteredEntitlementConnector(streamingConnector, ownerConnector, balanceConnector, grantConnector, grantRepo, entitlementRepo, publisher, logger, tracer)

patterns.name: ResetEntitlementUsage runs inside transaction.Run
patterns.description: Balance reset, usage-reset write, hooks, and event publish all happen inside one transaction.Run(ctx, grantRepo, ...) closure for atomicity.
patterns.example: transaction.Run(ctx, e.grantRepo, func(ctx) (*EntitlementBalance, error) { e.balanceConnector.ResetUsageForOwner(...); e.publisher.Publish(ctx, event) })

patterns.name: ParseFromGenericEntitlement strict type guard
patterns.description: Every connector method calls ParseFromGenericEntitlement immediately, validating required fields (MeasureUsageFrom, IsSoftLimit, UsagePeriod, LastReset, CurrentUsagePeriod) and returning WrongTypeError on mismatch.
patterns.example: mEnt, err := ParseFromGenericEntitlement(ent); if err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: EndCurrentUsagePeriod requires active transaction
patterns.description: grant_owner_adapter.go EndCurrentUsagePeriod checks transaction.GetDriverFromContext and errors if no tx is present.
patterns.example: if _, err := transaction.GetDriverFromContext(ctx); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("end current usage period must be called in a transaction: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Emit EntitlementResetEventV3, never v1
patterns.description: EntitlementResetEvent (v1) is deprecated. New code emits EntitlementResetEventV3 which carries CustomerID; events implement marshaler.Event with a version-pinned EventName.
patterns.example: e.publisher.Publish(ctx, EntitlementResetEventV3{EntitlementID: ent.ID, CustomerID: ent.CustomerID})

patterns.name: ServiceHookRegistry for cross-domain lifecycle
patterns.description: connector embeds models.ServiceHookRegistry[Entitlement]; calls hooks.PreUpdate before mutations and exposes RegisterHooks for balance-worker registration.
patterns.example: if err := e.hooks.PreUpdate(ctx, metered); err != nil { return EntitlementGrant{}, err }

patterns.name: queryMeter zero-length short-circuit
patterns.description: queryMeter returns a synthetic zero row when params.From==params.To without calling ClickHouse, avoiding degenerate edge-case queries.
patterns.example: if params.From != nil && params.To != nil && params.From.Equal(*params.To) { return []meter.MeterQueryRow{{Value: 0, WindowStart: *params.From, WindowEnd: *params.To}}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: Connector interface, struct, constructor. AfterCreate issues a default grant when HasDefaultGrant() is true.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AfterCreate issues the default grant outside a transaction — ensure idempotency if it may be called twice.

key_file_guides.role: GetEntitlementBalance / GetEntitlementBalanceHistory. Rounds up to nearest minute; fills zero-usage windows ClickHouse omits.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The zero-window fill loop must filter rows before MeasureUsageFrom or it returns stale windows.

key_file_guides.role: ResetEntitlementUsage and batch ResetEntitlementsWithExpiredUsagePeriod inside transaction.Run.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Batch reset uses errors.Join to collect per-entitlement errors but keeps iterating — partial failures are expected.
getRepoMaybeInTx rebinds featureRepo to ctx tx; LockOwnerForTx and EndCurrentUsagePeriod require an active transaction.

key_file_guides.role: EntitlementResetEvent (deprecated v1) and EntitlementResetEventV3, both marshaler.Event with pinned EventName.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New events need Validate() and a versioned EventType whose subsystem aligns with watermill/eventbus routing.
Keep Validate() in sync with openmeter/ent/schema constraints; the adapter calls it before insert.

anti_patterns: Bypassing transaction.Run in ResetEntitlementUsage — reset and event publish must be atomic.
Emitting EntitlementResetEvent v1 from new code — always use V3.
Calling EndCurrentUsagePeriod outside a transaction context.
Adding credit-engine logic directly to connector methods instead of via balanceConnector/grantConnector.
Using context.Background() in production connector methods.

decisions.decision: Balance history fills zero-usage windows missing from ClickHouse results.
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse only returns non-zero rows; the API contract requires an entry for every time bucket.

decisions.decision: ownerCustomer adapter wraps customer data without importing openmeter/customer into credit.
decisions.rationale: Prevents circular imports while satisfying the streaming.Customer interface required by grant.Owner.

decisions.decision: IssueAfterReset amount>0 triggers AfterCreate to issue a default recurring grant.
decisions.rationale: Lets operators configure a standing balance that replenishes each usage period without manual grant ops.

code_examples.scenario: Wire and call the metered connector in tests
code_examples.code:
connector := meteredentitlement.NewMeteredEntitlementConnector(streamingConnector, ownerConnector, creditConnector, creditConnector, grantRepo, entitlementRepo, mockPublisher, testLogger, tracer)
connector.RegisterHooks(meteredentitlement.ConvertHook(subscriptionHook))
balance, err := connector.GetEntitlementBalance(ctx, models.NamespacedID{Namespace: ns, ID: entID}, time.Now())

openmeter/entitlement/service.purpose: Orchestration layer implementing entitlement.Service by composing the three sub-type connectors (metered, boolean, static), the feature connector, customer service, and a distributed locker — routes each operation to the correct sub-type.

patterns.name: ServiceConfig constructor
patterns.description: NewEntitlementService takes a ServiceConfig struct with all deps explicit (Wire-built). No validation or logic in the constructor.
patterns.example: service.NewEntitlementService(service.ServiceConfig{MeteredEntitlementConnector: meteredConn, EntitlementRepo: entRepo, Locker: locker})

patterns.name: transaction.Run for composite mutations
patterns.description: CreateEntitlement and OverrideEntitlement wrap ScheduleEntitlement plus the grant-issuance loop in transaction.Run(ctx, entitlementRepo, ...) for cross-connector atomicity.
patterns.example: transaction.Run(ctx, c.entitlementRepo, func(ctx) (*entitlement.Entitlement, error) { ent, err := c.ScheduleEntitlement(ctx, input); ... })

patterns.name: ServiceHookRegistry fan-out
patterns.description: service embeds models.ServiceHookRegistry[entitlement.Entitlement] and exposes RegisterHooks; balance-worker registers hooks. Call hooks.PreUpdate before mutations.
patterns.example: s.hooks.RegisterHooks(hooks...)

patterns.name: registrybuilder for test setup
patterns.description: Tests obtain a fully wired registry via registrybuilder.GetEntitlementRegistry(...) instead of importing app/common, avoiding cycles.
patterns.example: registrybuilder.GetEntitlementRegistry(registrybuilder.EntitlementOptions{DatabaseClient: dbClient, StreamingConnector: streamingConnector})

patterns.name: Sub-type dispatch via type switch
patterns.description: ScheduleEntitlement switches on EntitlementType to call the right connector's BeforeCreate. Handlers must go through entitlement.Service, never connectors directly.
patterns.example: switch input.EntitlementType { case entitlement.EntitlementTypeMetered: return c.meteredConnector.BeforeCreate(input, feat) }

patterns.name: WithLock after tx opens
patterns.description: lock.go WithLock acquires pg_advisory_xact_lock per entitlement/customer via lockr.Locker, requiring the active Postgres tx from transaction.Run.
patterns.example: WithLock(ctx, locker, key, func(ctx) error { ... })

key_file_guides.role: Concrete entitlement.Service dispatching operations to sub-type connectors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateEntitlement delegates to ScheduleEntitlement then loops grants; if scheduling errors no grants are issued — keep this ordering.

key_file_guides.role: ScheduleEntitlement, SupersedeEntitlement, and time-window activation for scheduling future/expiring entitlements.
key_file_guides.watch_for: SupersedeEntitlement deletes the old and creates a new entitlement in the same transaction.Run closure.

key_file_guides.role: WithLock helper acquiring pg_advisory_xact_lock via lockr.Locker before mutation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Locker requires an active Postgres tx — call after transaction.Run opens one, not before.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common in test helpers — use registrybuilder.
Calling sub-type connector methods directly from HTTP handlers.
Adding DB queries to service.go — persistence goes through EntitlementRepo.
Calling lockr.Locker.LockForTX outside an active transaction.Run closure.

decisions.decision: Service is a thin orchestrator delegating to three independent sub-type connectors.
decisions.rationale: Each entitlement type has different storage/computation needs; centralizing dispatch avoids routing logic scattered across HTTP handlers.

openmeter/entitlement/snapshot.purpose: Defines SnapshotEvent — the versioned Watermill CloudEvent (entitlement.snapshot v2) published to the system topic on entitlement balance changes (reset, update, delete), consumed by the balance-worker and notification-service.

patterns.name: marshaler.Event with pinned version
patterns.description: SnapshotEvent implements marshaler.Event (EventName, EventMetadata, Validate); EventName uses metadata.GetEventName pinned to v2. New versions bump the version and keep the old struct.
patterns.example: snapshotEventName = metadata.GetEventName(metadata.EventType{Subsystem: entitlement.EventSubsystem, Name: "entitlement.snapshot", Version: "v2"})

patterns.name: NewSnapshotEvent constructor — no struct literals
patterns.description: Use NewSnapshotEvent() which handles a nil Subject safely and populates Namespace from the entitlement.
patterns.example: snapshot.NewSnapshotEvent(ent, subj, customer, feat, snapshot.ValueOperationUpdate, &calculatedAt, &value, &currentUsagePeriod)

patterns.name: Validate enforces Value for update/reset
patterns.description: Validate() errors if Value is nil for ValueOperationUpdate or ValueOperationReset; delete allows nil Value. CalculatedAt is always required. Call before publishing.
patterns.example: case ValueOperationUpdate, ValueOperationReset: if e.Value == nil { errs = append(errs, errors.New("balance is required")) }

key_file_guides.role: SnapshotEvent, EntitlementValue (balance/usage/overage/access/config), ValueOperationType enum, NewSnapshotEvent constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Subject field is deprecated — new consumer code reads Customer.ID. NewSnapshotEvent sets an empty Subject{} when subj is nil.

anti_patterns: Creating SnapshotEvent struct literals that omit CalculatedAt — Validate() rejects it.
Bumping the event version in place instead of adding a new versioned struct alongside the old.
Reading the Subject field in new consumer code — use Customer.
Publishing SnapshotEvent without calling Validate() first.

decisions.decision: SnapshotEvent v2 carries both deprecated Subject and Customer.
decisions.rationale: Edge workers were originally subject-based; migrating to customer-centric routing requires both fields during the transition.
